description: SQL is the language I think in when data is involved — writing complex queries, subqueries, window functions, and CTEs to extract exactly the insights a system needs to validate its data layer.
MySQL is my most battle-tested relational database — I design schemas, tune queries, manage replication, and write complex stored procedures for high-traffic production applications.
PostgreSQL is my relational database of choice for complex systems — JSONB columns, CTEs, advanced indexing, and rich extension support make it incredibly powerful for data-intensive applications.
MongoDB is my go-to for document-oriented data — I design flexible schemas, build aggregation pipelines, and validate data consistency across collections in large-scale NoSQL environments.
Oracle is where I work when enterprise scale demands it — PL/SQL packages, partitioned tables, and Oracle-specific optimizer hints are all part of my toolkit for mission-critical systems.
I use SQL Server for Microsoft-stack enterprise projects — T-SQL scripting, SSMS profiling, execution plan analysis, and Integration Services pipelines are central to my workflow there.
SQLite is my lightweight testing database — perfect for unit testing data layers, seeding isolated test environments, and validating schema migrations without spinning up a full database server.
I test Redis as a data store, not just a cache — validating TTL expiry behavior, pub/sub message delivery, sorted set operations, and data persistence configurations under load.

Cassandra testing demands a distributed mindset — I validate eventual consistency behavior, partition key design, compaction strategies, and read/write performance under multi-node cluster conditions.
JDBC is my Java bridge to any relational database — I use it to write database test utilities, execute parameterized queries, and validate result sets programmatically in automated test frameworks.
I write and test stored procedures as first-class application components — unit testing business logic encapsulated in the database layer with boundary cases, error handling, and rollback scenarios.
Database triggers are invisible logic that I make visible through testing — validating that BEFORE and AFTER triggers fire correctly, maintain audit trails, and never silently corrupt data.

I treat database views as testable contracts — validating that materialized and standard views return accurate, performant data and correctly abstract underlying table complexity from application layers.

Index strategy is one of my strongest database skills — I analyze execution plans, identify missing indexes, test covering index effectiveness, and measure the real query performance impact of each change.

I write and validate complex multi-table joins daily — testing INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL OUTER, and CROSS joins for correctness, null handling, and cartesian product edge cases that break applications silently.

I test transaction boundaries rigorously — validating ACID properties, deadlock handling, savepoint rollbacks, and isolation level behavior under concurrent access to ensure data never ends up in a corrupt state.

Data integrity is non-negotiable in every system I test — I validate foreign key constraints, unique constraints, check constraints, and cascading rules to ensure the database never accepts invalid state.

I validate database schemas against design specifications — checking column types, nullability, default values, and constraint definitions to catch structural drift before it reaches production environments.

ETL testing is about trusting your data pipeline — I validate source-to-target transformations, reconciliation counts, data type mappings, and null handling at every stage of the extraction and load process.

I test data migrations with surgical precision — pre and post-migration row counts, checksum comparisons, referential integrity checks, and rollback validation to ensure zero data loss during cutover.

I hunt slow queries like a detective — reading execution plans, identifying sequential scans, rewriting inefficient joins, and measuring before-and-after performance gains with cold and warm cache comparisons.
I benchmark database performance under realistic load — measuring query throughput, connection pool saturation, lock contention, and cache hit ratios to identify bottlenecks before they impact users.
I use TestNG to organize database test suites with group-based execution, dependency chains, and parallel data provider runs that validate hundreds of SQL scenarios in a single automated pass.
JUnit powers my database unit tests — I use it with in-memory databases and transaction rollback annotations to test SQL logic in complete isolation without leaving any test data behind.

DBUnit is my database state management tool for testing — seeding controlled datasets before tests, asserting exact table states after operations, and tearing down cleanly so tests never bleed into each other.
Liquibase keeps my database schema changes version-controlled and auditable — I test changeset rollbacks, validate checksums, and integrate schema migrations directly into CI deployment pipelines.

Flyway manages my database migration lifecycle with versioned SQL scripts — I validate migration ordering, test baseline scenarios, and ensure clean forward and repair operations across all environments.

I build and use data generators to seed realistic test datasets — production-like volumes with varied edge cases that expose bugs only visible when databases contain millions of properly distributed rows.

SQL Profiler is my forensic tool for slow query investigation — capturing execution traces, identifying N+1 patterns, and measuring the real cost of every database call an application makes under load.

I test backup and recovery processes as rigorously as application features — validating backup completeness, measuring recovery time objectives, and confirming point-in-time restore works before disaster strikes.

Database security testing is a dedicated practice for me — I audit user privilege assignments, test for SQL injection vectors, validate encrypted column handling, and verify audit logging captures all sensitive operations.
I validate database replication setups by testing lag tolerances, failover switchover times, replica consistency, and conflict resolution to ensure read replicas never serve stale or divergent data.

NoSQL testing requires thinking beyond rows and columns — I validate document structure consistency, key expiry behavior, collection-level access controls, and eventual consistency guarantees across distributed NoSQL systems.

I write database automation scripts that do the heavy lifting — seeding test data, resetting environment states, running health checks, generating comparison reports, and integrating DB validation into every CI pipeline I own.
